info.title: CallRaven API
info.description:
REST API for a containerized SIP softphone endpoint. Supports SIP registration, inbound/outbound calls, hold/resume, blind and attended transfer, 3-way conference, WAV file playback into the RTP stream, on-demand call recording, live RTP statistics, per-call packet capture (full SIP dialog + RTP), call history, and system logging.

All endpoints accept and return `application/json` unless stated otherwise. Real-time events are delivered via WebSocket at `ws://<host>:<port>` (control) and decoded PCM audio at `ws://<host>:<port>/audio` (live audio relay).

tags.name: WAV Playback
tags.description: Upload and play WAV audio into the RTP stream

tags.name: Captures
tags.description: Per-call pcap and audio recording download

tags.name: Recording
tags.description: On-demand call recording (separate from always-on pcap capture)

tags.name: Statistics
tags.description: Live RTP stats for the active call

get.description: Returns the full current state of the endpoint including registration status, active call, incoming call, and recent log entries.

post.description: Initiates SIP registration over WebSocket transport. On success a `registered` WebSocket event is broadcast. Registration is re-attempted automatically before expiry.
Sends a SIP REGISTER with Expires: 0 and stops the UA. A `unregistered` WebSocket event is broadcast.

Dials the target extension or SIP URI. A per-call packet capture (.pcap) and audio recording (.wav) are started automatically. Returns a `callId` which can be used to correlate captures and history entries.

**Target formats accepted:**
- `1002` → resolved to `sip:1002@<registered server>`
- `1002@pbx.local` → `sip:1002@pbx.local`
- `sip:alice@pbx.local` → used verbatim
Answers the current incoming call. A packet capture and audio recording are started. A `callConnected` WebSocket event is broadcast when the call is established.
Sends a SIP BYE and ends the active call. The packet capture and audio recording are finalised. A `callEnded` WebSocket event is broadcast.
Rejects the current incoming call with SIP 603 Decline.
Sends a DTMF digit via RFC 2833 telephone-event RTP during an active call.
Mutes the RTP bridge and sends a SIP re-INVITE with `a=sendonly` to signal hold to the remote party. A `callHeld` WebSocket event is broadcast.
Restores RTP forwarding and sends a SIP re-INVITE with `a=sendrecv` to resume the call. A `callResumed` WebSocket event is broadcast.
Sends a SIP REFER to the active call directing the remote party to call the target. The call leg from this endpoint ends immediately once the REFER is accepted.

Dials the transfer target as a second call leg. Once the target answers, sends a SIP REFER with Replaces header on the original leg, connecting the two remote parties directly. Both legs then end from this endpoint's perspective.
Dials a third party and bridges their RTP stream alongside the active call, creating a 3-way conference. A `conferenceStarted` WebSocket event is broadcast when the conference leg connects.
Terminates the conference leg only. The original two-party call remains active. A `conferenceEnded` WebSocket event is broadcast.

get.description: Returns all uploaded and converted WAV/G.722 files available for playback.

post.description: Accepts any WAV file (any sample rate, channels, bit depth). ffmpeg converts it to raw G.722 16kHz mono at upload time. The converted file is stored as `<name>.g722` and returned for use with `/api/play`.

delete.description: Deletes a WAV/G.722 file from the server.

post.summary: Play WAV file into active call
post.description: Injects a pre-converted G.722 file into the active RTP stream. Playback takes over the stream's SSRC and sequence number for seamless injection. Incoming RTP is suppressed during playback. A `playbackEnded` WebSocket event is broadcast when complete.
Stops any active WAV playback and resumes normal RTP forwarding. A `playbackEnded` WebSocket event is broadcast.

get.description: Returns all saved .pcap files. Each entry optionally includes a link to a decoded audio .wav recording of the inbound RTP stream for that call.

delete.description: Deletes a .pcap or audio .wav file from the captures directory.

get.description: Returns the full call history as JSON, most recent first. History is persisted to `/captures/call_history.json` and survives container restarts.
Downloads the full call history as a CSV file suitable for import into spreadsheet applications.
Downloads the current in-memory system log as a plain text file with timestamps.
Returns live RTP statistics for the active call. Updated in real time by the RTP bridge. Returns 409 if no call is active.

post.description: Begins recording the inbound audio of the active call to a WAV file in `/captures`. This is separate from the always-on pcap capture and can be started and stopped at any point during a call. A `recordingStarted` WebSocket event is broadcast.
Stops the active recording and saves the WAV file. The file is immediately available in `/captures`. A `recordingStopped` WebSocket event is broadcast with the download path.

target.description: Extension number, user@host, or full SIP URI
Extension number, user@host, or full SIP URI

SIPConfig.description: Registration configuration used by the current UA

remoteHold.description: True if far end has put the call on hold (a=sendonly re-INVITE)

recording.description: True if on-demand recording is currently active

codec.description: Negotiated RTP codec (populated once RTP is flowing)

callId.description: Pre-assigned call ID for tracking missed calls in history

status.description: missed = inbound rang but not answered; cancelled = outbound hung up pre-answer

Conflict.description: Operation not allowed in current state (e.g. already on a call)

WebSocketEvents.description: Connect to ws://<host>:<port> to receive real-time events. On connect the current state is sent immediately as a `state` event.
